LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRAWFORD AND COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT BEFORE THE COURT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF MUST SEEK LEAVE OF COURT TO FILE IT. DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FIVE, ELEVEN, TWELVE AND SEVENTEEN. CRAWFORD IS NOT A STATE ACTOR UNDER 42 USC SECTION 1983; THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR OBSTRUCTION OF JUSTICE; PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OPPOSED THE DEMURRER TO THE TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND CONCEDES THE SEVENTEENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS DEFECTIVE. THE FOLLOWING ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN THIRTY DAYS: CAUSES OF ACTION FOUR AND NINE TO ALLEGE EXPRESS MISREPRESENTATION TO PLAINTIFF BY CRAWFORD; CAUSE OF ACTION FIFTEEN TO ALLEGE HOW CRAWFORD'S ACTS PROXIMATELY CAUSED DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF'S PERSON OR PROPERTY. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY OF FRESNO, FRESNO FIRE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FOLLOWING CAUSES OF ACTION: THE 4TH FOR CONSPIRACY, THE 5TH FOR DUE PROCESS AND THE 6TH FOR CONSTITUTIONAL VIOLATIONS. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF SOUGHT MENTAL HEALTH TREATMENT IN OCTOBER 2003. BY THAT TIME OVER TWO YEARS HAD PASSED SINCE PLAINTIFF WAS THE SUBJECT OF A CRIMINAL COMPLAINT. UNDER CCP SECTION 335.1, THE APPLICABLE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAD RUN. OWENS V. OKURAE (1989) 488 U.S. 235. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD FACTS THAT DEMONSTRATE THAT THE CLAIM PERIOD WAS TOLLED. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT THE TIME TO FILE A GOVERNMENT CLAIM WAS TOLLED BY MENTAL INCAPACITY. THIS RULING APPLIES TO THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF PRIVACY, 13TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR OBSTRUCTION OF JUSTICE AND THE 14TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MISREPRESENTATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. PARTIES WERE ASSESSED PER DIEM FEES. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRAWFORD AND COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOLLOWING CAUSES OF ACTION: 8TH, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, 9TH, RIGHT TO PRIVACY AND 14TH, MISREPRESENTATION, BECAUSE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION WERE ADDED WITHOUT SEEKING LEAVE OF COURT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSPIRACY AND 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. THE 16TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CRAWFORD OWED NO DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF. SANCHEZ V. LINDSEY MORDEN CLAIM SERVICES, INC. (1999) 72 CAL.APP.4TH 249, 253-255. COOPERATING WITH LAW ENFORCEMENT IS NOT ACTIONABLE CONDUCT. ARNOLD V. IBM (9TH CIR. 1981) 637 F.2D 1350. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. PARTIES WERE ASSESSED PER DIEM FEES. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FOLLOWING CAUSES OF ACTION: 8TH INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, 9TH PRIVACY, 14TH MISREPRESENTATION, 15TH CLAIMS ACT, 16 NEGLIGENT INVESTIGATION, 19TH CONVERSION AND 20 CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST. LEAVE TO AMEND WAS GRANTED AS TO THE 4TH, 10TH AND 12TH CAUSES OF ACTION. NO OPPOSITION WAS ASSERTED AS TO THE 15TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS NO DUTY OF CARE APPLICABLE TO THE CARRIER AND SO THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM FAILS. SEE CROSKEY & HEESEMAN, CAL. PRAC. GUIDE: INSURANCE LITIGATION (THE RUTTER GROUP) SECTION 11.205, P.11-48. A CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST MAY NOT BE IMPOSED ON FUNDS THAT DEFENDANT ADMITTEDLY DOES NOT POSSESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. PARTIES WERE ASSESSED PER DIEM FEES. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD AND BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THE ADMISSIONS IN THE 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE FORCED POLICY SHOW THAT THE POLICY WAS SOLEY FOR OPTION ONE'S BENEFIT. THE FRAUD CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE IT LACKS PARTICULARITY AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT ALLEGE A NEXUS BETWEEN THE ALLEGED FRAUD AND HIS HARM. FURTHER, IT IS UNCLEAR HOW PLAINTIFF RELIED. FURTHER, THE INSURANCE CODE REQUIRED THE INSURER TO COOPERATE WITH GOVERNMENT INVESTIGATIONS. AS TO THE 1983 CLAIMS, WHILE DEFENDANTS WERE NOT PART OF THE FEDERAL ACTION, THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL PRECLUDES A PARTY TO PRIOR LITIGATION FROM DISPUTING ISSUES DECIDED THEREIN AGAINST HIM, EVEN WHEN THOSE ISSUES BEAR ON DIFFERENT CLAIMS RAISED IN A LATER CASE. SMITH V. EXXON MOBILE OIL CORP. (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 1407, 1414. BECAUSE THE FEDERAL COURT ALREADY RULED THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT ALLEGE A 4TH AMENDMENT ILLEGAL SEARCH, HE IS BARRED FROM RAISING IT HERE BASED UPON THE SAME FACTS UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF RES JUDICATA AND COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Gregory Morris, 706-1589 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Victoria Robinson Smith, Esq. of Law Offices of Victoria Robinson Smith 925/253-1844 for defts Option One Mortgage Corporation & Premier Trust Deed Services; Lori A. Sebransky, Esq. of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP 510/433-2600 for defts Crawford & Company and General Insurance Company of America.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD AND BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THE ADMISSIONS IN THE 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE FORCED POLICY SHOW THAT THE POLICY WAS SOLEY FOR OPTION ONE'S BENEFIT. THE FRAUD CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE IT LACKS PARTICULARITY AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT ALLEGE A NEXUS BETWEEN THE ALLEGED FRAUD AND HIS HARM. FURTHER, IT IS UNCLEAR HOW PLAINTIFF RELIED. FURTHER, THE INSURANCE CODE REQUIRED THE INSURER TO COOPERATE WITH GOVERNMENT INVESTIGATIONS. AS TO THE 1983 CLAIMS, WHILE DEFENDANTS WERE NOT PART OF THE FEDERAL ACTION, THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL PRECLUDES A PARTY TO PRIOR LITIGATION FROM DISPUTING ISSUES DECIDED THEREIN AGAINST HIM, EVEN WHEN THOSE ISSUES BEAR ON DIFFERENT CLAIMS RAISED IN A LATER CASE. SMITH V. EXXON MOBILE OIL CORP. (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 1407, 1414. BECAUSE THE FEDERAL COURT ALREADY RULED THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT ALLEGE A 4TH AMENDMENT ILLEGAL SEARCH, HE IS BARRED FROM RAISING IT HERE BASED UPON THE SAME FACTS UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF RES JUDICATA AND COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY OF FRESNO, FRESNO FIRE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Michael McGinnis of Mac McGinnis (559.497.7979) for plaintiff and responding party
Gregory Myers of the City of Fresno's Attorney's Office (559.621.7500) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: The demurrer is sustained as to the following causes of action: the 4th for conspiracy, the 5th for due process and the 6th for constitutional violations. The complaint alleges that plaintiff sought mental health treatment in October 2003. By that time over two years had passed since plaintiff was the subject of a criminal complaint. Under CCP section 335.1, the applicable two year statute of limitations had run. Owens v. Okurae (1989) 488 U.S. 235. Leave to amend is granted for plaintiff to plead facts that demonstrate that the claim period was tolled.
Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to plead facts demonstrating that the time to file a government claim was tolled by mental incapacity. This ruling applies to the 9th cause of action for violation of privacy, 13th cause of action for obstruction of justice and the 14th cause of action for misrepresentation. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Parties were assessed per diem fees. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
